name: Gulf Cooperation Council
climate: Arid desert, extremely hot summers exceeding 50°C, mild winters, very low rainfall

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: GCC members border the Persian Gulf; shared maritime space and oil export routes

neighbors.name: Strait of Hormuz
neighbors.relevance: Primary oil and LNG export chokepoint for GCC producers

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Saudi Arabia's western coast; alternative export route and NEOM development corridor

neighbors.name: Levant
neighbors.relevance: Northern neighbor; GCC investment destination and refugee crisis spillover

neighbors.name: Horn of Africa
neighbors.relevance: Across the Red Sea; GCC military bases in Djibouti and Eritrea, labor migration source

neighbors.name: OPEC
neighbors.relevance: SAU, ARE, KWT are core OPEC members; production quota coordination

links.description: Saudi Arabia leads OPEC; UAE and Kuwait are key members setting global oil policy
GCC states anchor OPEC+ production coordination with Russia and other producers
Over 80% of GCC oil exports transit the Strait of Hormuz
Saudi Red Sea coast provides alternative export route and Vision 2030 development axis
SAU and ARE joined BRICS in 2024, diversifying geopolitical alignments
Major BRI partners; Chinese infrastructure investment and oil-for-development deals
Asia is the primary destination for GCC oil and gas exports
